query: Italian Navy minehunters Crotone Rimini Suez Canal transit May 2026 deployment status

# Italian Navy Minehunters Crotone & Rimini: Suez Canal Transit and May 2026 Deployment Status

## Executive Summary

The Italian Navy minehunters **Crotone (M 5558)** and **Rimini (M 5561)**, both Gaeta-class mine countermeasures vessels (MCMVs), departed **Augusta, Sicily on 15 May 2026** for forward deployment to the Middle East. Their mission is to participate in an international coalition effort to clear naval mines from the **Strait of Hormuz**, which Iran had mined during an ongoing military conflict with the United States and Israel. As of the reporting date (18 May 2026, Naval News article), the vessels were in transit; their destination is expected to be **Djibouti**, where Italy maintains a forward operating base, with an estimated two-week transit time. The transit route would necessarily include passage through the **Suez Canal** en route to the Red Sea and onward to Djibouti/the Gulf region.

## Ship Details

### Crotone (M 5558)
- **Class:** Gaeta-class (improved Lerici-class) coastal minehunter
- **Builder:** Intermarine, Sarzana, Italy (built 1992-1996)
- **Hull:** GRP (Glass-Reinforced Plastics) — non-magnetic
- **Key systems:** Thales 2093 Variable Depth Sonar (VDS), Leonardo MCM C2 suite, Pluto Plus and Pluto Gigas ROV/AUV (up to 600m depth), REMUS 100 and REMUS 300 AUVs (with Kraken Robotics SAS), hyperbaric chamber for diver detachment
- **Command:** Mine Countermeasures Forces Command (MARICODRAG), 5th Naval Division

### Rimini (M 5561)
- **Class:** Gaeta-class — same as Crotone; last unit of the class
- **Special modifications:** Fitted to embark one 20-foot and one 10-foot container as launch/recovery/C2 module for the **Kongsberg Maritime HUGIN AUV** (operates to 3,000m depth); the Command received a second HUGIN in late 2023
- **Command:** Same as Crotone (MARICODRAG)

## Deployment Context: The Strait of Hormuz Mine Crisis

### Background
- A military conflict between the **US-Israel axis and Iran** erupted in early 2026 (referenced as starting approximately early March 2026).
- **Iran mined the Strait of Hormuz**, through which approximately **20% of global oil transits**.
- As of May 2026, approximately **1,500 ships with 20,000 crew members** were blocked in the Persian Gulf due to Hormuz shipping disruptions (per the IMO Secretary-General).
- A Pentagon report to Congress (cited by the Washington Post) estimated it could take **up to six months** to fully clear the mines from the Strait.
- The US attempted to launch "Operation Project Freedom" to escort neutral ships through Hormuz starting 4 May, but it collapsed within 48 hours due to objections from Gulf Arab monarchies.

### International Coalition Planning
- On **22 April 2026**, over **30 countries** met at the **Permanent Joint Headquarters at Northwood** (UK) to plan a multinational mine-clearance and maritime protection mission.
- The mission is envisioned as **Franco-British led**, with "strictly defensive" objectives focused on merchant shipping protection and demining.
- Italian PM **Giorgia Meloni** attended the Paris-level political meeting alongside UK PM Keir Starmer, French President Macron, and German Chancellor Friedrich Merz.

## Italian Deployment Details (May 2026)

### Departure & Route
- **Departure date:** 15 May 2026, from **Augusta, Sicily**
- **Destination:** Expected to be **Djibouti** (Italian Armed Forces forward operating base)
- **Estimated transit time:** Approximately **two weeks** (arrival ~late May/early June 2026)
- **Route:** Mediterranean → Suez Canal → Red Sea → Gulf of Aden → Djibouti (implied transit corridor)

### Phased Positioning (per Defence Minister Crosetto)
Italian Defence Minister **Guido Crosetto** explained the phased approach on 13 May 2026:
1. **Initially:** Position in the **Eastern Mediterranean**
2. **Subsequently:** Move to the **Red Sea**, within the frameworks of ongoing missions "Mediterraneo Sicuro" (Secure Mediterranean) and "Aspides"
3. **Ultimately:** Position closer to the **Strait of Hormuz** once Parliament authorizes the mission under an international legal framework and a stable truce is reached

### Task Group Composition (planned)
According to Naval News and the Italian Navy Chief of Staff Admiral **Giuseppe Berutti Bergotto**, the planned task group includes **four ships total**:

| Ship | Type | Status |
|------|------|--------|
| **Crotone (M 5558)** | Gaeta-class MCMV | Departed 15 May 2026 |
| **Rimini (M 5561)** | Gaeta-class MCMV | Departed 15 May 2026 |
| **Raimondo Montecuccoli (P 432)** | Thaon di Revel-class PPA "Light Plus" (air defence escort) | Expected to follow (timing unspecified) |
| **Atlante (A 5336)** | Vulcano-class Logistic Support Ship (LSS) | Expected to follow (timing unspecified) |

### Personnel
- **MARICODRAG** crew on both MCMVs
- **COMSUBIN** personnel: GOS (Gruppo Operativo Subacquei) divers with EOD capabilities (60m qualified) + GOI (Gruppo Operativo Incursori) special forces operators
- **San Marco Marine Brigade** operators

### Legal/Political Framework
- Italian participation awaits **Parliamentary authorization**
- Italy will contribute once a "credible and stable truce" is obtained
- The mission must operate under a "legitimate international legal framework"
- Public opinion polling: **57.2% of Italians opposed** sending minehunters to Hormuz, 28.1% in favor, 14.7% undecided (per 'Only Numbers'/Ghisleri polling institute)

## Prior Operations: Operation Noble Shield (2025)

Both vessels have recent NATO operational experience:
- On **7 September 2025**, during **Operation Noble Shield**, Nave Rimini completed a handover to Nave Crotone after **over 60 days** of operations under NATO command as part of the **Standing Naval Forces (SNF)** mine countermeasures group.
- Rimini patrolled strategic areas of the **central-western Mediterranean**, participated in exercises with allied navies, and contributed to monitoring major maritime routes.
- Crotone took over having recently achieved **MAR.EVAL. certification** (operational readiness assessment).
- The handover was overseen by **Admiral Traetta**, Commander of the **5th Naval Division** (Quinta Divisione Navale).
- In **March 2026**, both were involved in training as part of the **54th Coastal Minesweeper Squadron** exercises.

## Historical Precedent
Italy's MARICODRAG Command previously participated in Middle East mine-clearing operations:
- **"Operation Golfo 1"** (1987-1988) — clearing Iranian mines from the Persian Gulf
- **"Operation Golfo 2"** (1991) — Persian Gulf mine clearance

## Key Uncertainties
- **Suez Canal transit:** The article confirms the ships departed Sicily on 15 May with Djibouti as destination (~2-week transit), which necessarily involves Suez Canal passage, but no specific date for the canal transit was reported.
- **Escort/logistics timing:** The Montecuccoli and Atlante departure dates were not confirmed.
- **Parliamentary authorization:** As of 18 May 2026, Italy's Parliament had not yet authorized the operational mission.
- **Ceasefire status:** The deployment is contingent on a stable truce being reached between the US-Israel coalition and Iran.

---

sources.title: Italy is forward-deploying mine countermeasures assets in the Middle East
sources.snippet: The two mine countermeasures vessels that Italy is forward-deploying in the Middle East area to participate to the international coalition to make the Strait of Hormuz free for navigation again, have left Augusta in Sicily on 15 May. By Luca Peruzzi, published 18/05/2026.

sources.title: Passaggio di testimone tra Nave Rimini e Nave Crotone nell'Operazione Noble Shield
sources.snippet: Il 7 settembre 2025, nel contesto dell'Operazione Noble Shield, si è svolto il passaggio di consegne tra Nave Rimini, al termine di due mesi di intensa attività operativa, e Nave Crotone. Il cacciamine Rimini ha concluso il proprio impiego all'interno del gruppo di contromisure mine della Standing Naval Forces (SNF).

sources.title: Missione europea nel Golfo: mostrar bandiera per sembrare meno irrilevanti
sources.snippet: L'Italia si appresta a mettere a disposizione della missione 2 cacciamine classe Gaeta, una fregata lanciamissili e una nave logistica. Il capo di Stato maggiore della Marina, ammiraglio Berutti Bergotto, ha confermato 'quattro navi: due operative, una logistica e una di scorta'.

sources.title: Incertezze e instabilità dominano il Golfo Persico
sources.snippet: Circa 1.500 navi con 20.000 membri degli equipaggi sono bloccate nel Golfo a causa delle interruzioni del traffico nello Stretto di Hormuz. US Operation Project Freedom collapsed within 48 hours due to Gulf Arab objections.

sources.title: Addestramento di reparto delle navi della 54a squadriglia dragamine costieri
sources.snippet: March 2026 training exercises of the 54th Coastal Minesweeper Squadron including Crotone and Rimini.

key_facts: Italian Navy minehunters Crotone (M 5558) and Rimini (M 5561), both Gaeta-class MCMVs, departed Augusta, Sicily on 15 May 2026 for forward deployment to the Middle East.
The mission objective is to participate in an international coalition to clear naval mines from the Strait of Hormuz, mined by Iran during an ongoing conflict with the US and Israel.
The expected destination is Djibouti, where Italy maintains a forward operating base, with an estimated two-week transit time — implying Suez Canal transit around mid-to-late May 2026.

Italian Defence Minister Guido Crosetto outlined a phased approach: initially Eastern Mediterranean, then Red Sea (under Mediterraneo Sicuro and Aspides frameworks), then closer to the Strait of Hormuz.
Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani announced the deployment to Parliament's Defence Committees on 13 May 2026.

The full planned task group includes 4 ships: two MCMVs (departed), escort PPA Raimondo Montecuccoli (P 432), and logistic support vessel Atlante (A 5336) — the latter two expected to follow at unspecified timing.
COMSUBIN special forces (GOI), divers (GOS with EOD capability), and San Marco Marine Brigade operators are expected to participate.
Italian participation is contingent on Parliamentary authorization and a stable ceasefire in the Iran conflict.
On 22 April 2026, over 30 countries met at Northwood (UK) to plan the multinational demining/maritime protection mission, envisioned as Franco-British led.
Italian Navy Chief of Staff Admiral Giuseppe Berutti Bergotto confirmed the Italian contribution would be 'four ships: two operational, one logistic, and one escort.'
Approximately 1,500 ships with 20,000 crew members were blocked in the Persian Gulf due to Hormuz disruptions as of May 2026 (per IMO).
A Pentagon report estimated up to 6 months could be needed to fully clear Hormuz mines.
In September 2025, both ships participated in Operation Noble Shield under NATO Standing Naval Forces, with Rimini completing 60+ days of NATO operations before handing over to Crotone.
57.2% of Italians opposed sending minehunters to Hormuz in polling by Only Numbers/Ghisleri institute.

Both Gaeta-class MCMVs underwent Mid-Life Updates and are equipped with Thales 2093 VDS, Leonardo C2 suite, Pluto Plus/Gigas ROVs, REMUS 100/300 AUVs, and Rimini can operate the Kongsberg HUGIN AUV to 3,000m depth.
